name: apache2
title: apache2
summary: a2enmod, a2dismod - enable or disable an apache2 module
description:
Apache HTTP Server
The Apache HTTP Server Project's goal is to build a secure, efficient and
extensible HTTP server as standards-compliant open source software. The
result has long been the number one web server on the Internet.
.
Installing this package results in a full installation, including the
configuration files, init scripts and support scripts.

commands.name: a2enmod
commands.description: enable or disable an apache2 module

arguments.description: When disabling a module, also cascade disables all modules that depends on it.
arguments.raw: -f  When disabling a module, also cascade disables all modules that depends on it.

arguments.description: When disabling a module, also cascade disables all modules that depends on it.
arguments.raw: --force  When disabling a module, also cascade disables all modules that depends on it.

arguments.description: Enables the maintainer mode, that is the program invocation is effectuated automati-
arguments.raw: -m  Enables the maintainer mode, that is the program invocation is effectuated automati-

arguments.description: Enables the maintainer mode, that is the program invocation is effectuated automati-
arguments.raw: --maintmode  Enables the maintainer mode, that is the program invocation is effectuated automati-

arguments.description: stance and override APACHE_MODS_AVAIL back to /etc/apache2/mods-available to have a
arguments.raw: -myin  stance and override APACHE_MODS_AVAIL back to /etc/apache2/mods-available to have a

arguments.description: disabling a module, purge all traces of the module in the internal state data

arguments.description: disabling a module, purge all traces of the module in the internal state data

commands.name: a2query
commands.description: retrieve runtime configuration from a local Apache 2 HTTP server

commands.name: a2disconf
commands.description: enable or disable an apache2 configuration file

commands.name: a2dismod
commands.description: enable or disable an apache2 module

commands.name: a2dissite
commands.description: enable or disable an apache2 site / virtual host

commands.name: a2enconf
commands.description: enable or disable an apache2 configuration file

commands.name: a2ensite
commands.description: enable or disable an apache2 site / virtual host

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use a2enmod because a2enmod, a2dismod - enable or disable an apache2 module.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use a2enmod as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `a2enmod [-q|--quiet] [-m|--maintmode] [ module ]` as documented upstream for a2enmod.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `a2enmod imagemap` as documented upstream for a2enmod.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair a2enmod with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `a2enmod --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
